pattern: Extend each starting index rightward maintaining a running gcd that only ever shrinks, counting the windows whose gcd lands exactly on k.
hints: The gcd of a growing window is monotonically non-increasing, so once it drops below k you can stop extending.
Fix the left end and fold each new element into a running gcd as you move the right end.
Every time the running gcd equals k, increment the answer; if it ever divides to less than k or stops being a multiple of k, break early.

edge_inputs.description: single element not divisible by k
all multiples of k but gcd of pairs exceeds k
k is one so every subarray with gcd one counts
values are multiples of k but none reduce exactly to k
large values whose gcd is exactly k

stress.description: values built as k times small factors so many windows reach gcd k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    k = rng.choice([1, 2, 3, 5])
    nums = [k * rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, k]
